title_en: Why Are Some Women Training for Pregnancy Like It’s a Marathon?
title_zh: 为什么有些女性把备孕当作马拉松来训练？

paragraphs.en: Three years ago, Esther Rohr and her husband decided to start thinking about pregnancy. The 26-year-old Oregon-based wedding photographer made small but intentional lifestyle changes—going to bed earlier, drinking more water and less alcohol, dialing in her fitness, loading up on protein, and taking supplements like beef organ capsules and Vitamin D3. They started charging their phones in the kitchen for better sleep and unplugging their Wi-Fi at night, because her research suggested it might affect cellular health. Concerned about their exposure to reproductive toxins, Rohr began the slow, painstaking task of swapping out all their synthetic workout clothes, nonstick pans, and scented personal care products that might contain phthalates or other endocrine-disrupting chemicals. She bought an air purifier and hopes to eventually replace their LED bulbs with incandescents, because she worries they might be affecting her circadian rhythm.
paragraphs.zh: 三年前，埃斯特·罗尔（Esther Rohr）和她的丈夫决定开始考虑怀孕。26岁的俄勒冈州婚礼摄影师做了一些小而有意图的生活调整——更早入睡、喝更多水并减少酒精摄入、把运动节奏调得更规律、增加蛋白质摄入，并开始服用牛脏器胶囊、维生素D3等补充剂。她开始把手机放在厨房里充电以改善睡眠，并在夜间关闭Wi-Fi，因为她的研究提示这可能影响细胞健康。担心接触到生殖毒素后，罗尔开始慢慢而辛苦地更换全部合成运动服、不粘锅以及可能含有邻苯二甲酸酯或其他内分泌干扰物的香味个人护理产品。她买了空气净化器，并希望最终把家里的LED灯泡换成白炽灯，因为她担心LED可能影响自己的昼夜节律。

paragraphs.en: Rohr has never tried to conceive before. To her knowledge, she doesn’t have any health problems that would make it difficult. But nonetheless, she’s dedicated to optimizing her health to give their baby the “best chance at a healthy start in life.” Learning from accounts like @vitallymelanie, and @innate_fertility, what began as a curious interest in “crunchy, healthy, fitness, baby mama” content turned into a timeline filled with guidance for easy, breezy, positive pregnancies—and the tools she should use to get there.
paragraphs.zh: 罗尔此前从未尝试过受孕。据她所知，她没有任何会导致受孕困难的健康问题。然而，即便如此，她仍致力于把自己的健康状态调到最佳，给即将到来的宝宝“创造一个最有利于健康起步的开局”。她从名为@vitallymelanie和@innate_fertility的账号中学习到内容后，那种最初只是出于好奇的“健康、健身、宝妈生活”方向的关注，最终转变成了一条充满“轻松、自然、积极怀孕”指导的时间线，以及她应使用的相关工具清单。

paragraphs.en: One of the accounts recommended reading 9 Months Is Not Enough, a book premised around the idea that women are plagued by a “fertility gap,” but not to worry because “you can do something about it.” That resonated with Rohr. After changing her house and her habits, Rohr also decided to go through extra hormone testing and a controversial OligoScan, which measures minerals and heavy metals in your body through a light-frequency-based hand scan. For the next year, she tried to lower the levels of mercury that showed up. Rohr and her husband completed two rounds of Dr. Daniel Pompa’s “cellular healing diet,” essentially a keto diet, and taking six to eight supplements each day.
paragraphs.zh: 其中一个账号推荐她阅读《9个月还不够》（9 Months Is Not Enough），这本书基于这样的观点：女性有一个“生育缺口”，但不必担心，因为“你可以采取行动加以改善”。这一点与罗尔的想法产生了共鸣。换了居住环境和生活习惯后，罗尔又决定进行额外的激素检测，并做了一次颇具争议的OligoScan，它通过基于光频技术的手部扫描来测量体内的矿物质和重金属含量。接下来的一年里，她尝试降低检测结果中出现的汞水平。罗尔和丈夫完成了两轮丹尼尔·庞帕医生（Dr. Daniel Pompa）的“细胞修复饮食”（cellular healing diet），本质上是一种生酮饮食，并且每天服用六到八种补充剂。

paragraphs.en: “It was a brutal, brutal year,” Rohr says. But it was all for a greater purpose. “I need to be focused on giving my body building blocks. Because right now, it's a huge, pivotal time to be giving it what it needs.” In late December of last year, after three years of preparation, Rohr finally feels like she’s on the precipice of being “ready.”
paragraphs.zh: “那是一段残酷、残酷的一年，”罗尔说，但这一切都有更大的目的。“我需要集中精力给身体提供构建所需的基础材料。因为现在，是给它真正需要的东西的时候，而且这是一个非常关键的时刻。”去年12月末，经过三年的准备后，罗尔终于觉得自己“快要准备就绪”了。

paragraphs.en: The cultural obsession with wellness and optimization, which is currently driven and designed by male biohackers like Bryan Johnson and Peter Attia, has come for this murky preconception period, coined “the zero trimester” by sociologist Miranda Waggoner in her 2017 book by the same name. Women have started training for pregnancy “like it’s a marathon,” as influencer Kaylie Stewart announced to her 1 million TikTok followers last fall. After all, if you spend months planning a wedding, some influencers point out, why wouldn’t you do the same for a baby?
paragraphs.zh: 围绕健康和“优化”的文化狂热——目前由像 Bryan Johnson 和 Peter Attia 这样的男性“生物黑客”推动并塑造——已经延伸到了模糊的受孕前阶段，这一阶段在社会学家 Miranda Waggoner 2017 年的同名著作中被称为“零胎期”。女性也开始像为马拉松比赛做准备那样为怀孕做准备，正如网红 Kaylie Stewart 去年秋天向她 100 万名 TikTok 粉丝宣布的那样。毕竟，如果你会为婚礼花上几个月精心筹划，正如一些网红所指出的，为什么不也同样为孩子做同样的准备呢？

paragraphs.en: A growing group of influencers and holistic women’s health experts, doctors, life coaches, and nutritionists are posting content that speaks to the “Trying to Conceive” (TTC) demographic—including women who are struggling to conceive and those who haven’t started yet. The concept is simple: If you follow this wellness formula, you will set yourself up for the quickest conception, the easiest pregnancy, and the healthiest child. Pregnancy, these accounts argue, doesn’t have to be traumatic. You get to be in the driver’s seat. And what mom-to-be wouldn’t want that?
paragraphs.zh: 越来越多的网红、整体女性健康专家、医生、生活教练和营养师正在发布面向“备孕”（Trying to Conceive，简称 TTC）人群的内容，包括正在备孕困难的女性以及尚未开始的人群。这个概念很简单：如果你遵循这套健康公式，就能为自己创造最快的受孕、最顺畅的怀孕过程和最健康的孩子。那些账号认为，怀孕并不一定是痛苦的经历。你可以掌握主动权。还有谁不希望准妈妈能拥有这种掌控感呢？

paragraphs.en: These days, #preconception appears in 106,000 Instagram posts and #pregnancyprep in 36,000. Singular TikTok “prep” videos rack up tens of thousands—sometimes millions—of views and likes. “Pregnancy Prep” influencers post curated lists, aesthetic vlogs, and GRWM TikToks filled with glowing, happy women. They suggest a litany of lifestyle changes, niche products, books, courses, and “quick” tips and tricks to follow in the 6 to 12 months before getting pregnant. “Healthy pregnancy isn’t chance—it’s choice,” is a favorite line of Alexandra Radway, 29, a functional nutritional therapy practitioner who has been posting pregnancy prep content on her Instagram account, @alexandraradway, since 2021.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，#preconception 出现在 106,000 篇 Instagram 帖子中，#pregnancyprep 出现在 36,000 篇帖子中。单条 TikTok 的“备孕”短视频常常能获得数万乃至数百万次浏览和点赞。做“孕期准备”的网红会发布精心挑选的清单、审美化的 vlog 以及充满光鲜快乐妈妈形象的 GRWM TikTok。他们在受孕前 6 到 12 个月内推荐大量生活方式调整、细分品类产品、读物、课程，以及各种“快速”小技巧和妙招。29 岁的功能性营养治疗从业者 Alexandra Radway 常说的一句最受欢迎的话是：“健康的怀孕不是偶然——是选择。” 她自 2021 年起在 Instagram 账号 @alexandraradway 上持续发布备孕内容。

paragraphs.en: Unsurprisingly, much of this content asks women to buy products and programs. From brazil nuts for “egg health” and grass-fed butter, to prenatal vitamins, pelvic floor Pilates, and nontoxic pans, the prepregnancy changeups and swap-outs offered up on social media seem never-ending. Some are basic, and others, more unconventional. One of Radway’s favorite tips is to watch the sun rise. “It's the equivalent of, like, 2,500 Brazil nuts of antioxidants,” she says. “Everyone's focusing on super foods. I'm like, ‘No, super sunrise.’”
paragraphs.zh: 毫不令人意外，这类内容中有很多都引导女性购买产品和课程。从为了“卵子健康”而吃巴西坚果、到食用草饲黄油，再到产前维生素、骨盆底普拉提课程，以及无毒平底锅，社交媒体上推出的“备孕”替换清单几乎看起来没有尽头。有些建议很基础，也有一些更不寻常。Radway 最喜欢的一条建议就是看日出。“这相当于补充了大约 2,500 颗巴西坚果那么多的抗氧化物，”她说。“每个人都在强调超级食物。我则想说：‘不，是超级日出。’”

paragraphs.en: Radway’s advice may sound woo-woo, but preconception health has been around since the beginning of time. Ancient cultures, like the Spartans, encouraged “maidens” to “exercise themselves with wrestling, running, throwing the quoit, and casting the dart” so that “the fruit they conceived might … take firmer root and find better growth.” For millennia, traditional Chinese medicine and Ayurveda have sought to boost fertility through diet, panchakarma, and acupuncture. There are even Biblical passages encouraging sobriety before conception.
paragraphs.zh: 拉德韦的建议听上去可能有点“玄”，但关于孕前健康的观念从古至今都存在。像斯巴达人这样的古代文化就鼓励“少女”进行“摔跤、跑步、掷铁饼和投掷飞镖”的训练，以便“她们所受孕的果实能够…更牢固地生根并获得更好的成长”。几千年来，传统中医和阿育吠陀都通过饮食、五体净化（panchakarma）和针灸来提高生育力。甚至《圣经》中也有鼓励在受孕前保持节制的段落。

paragraphs.en: In Western medicine, several governing medical bodies, including the American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists, published the first guidelines for perinatal care in 1983, stating that “preparation for parenthood should begin prior to conception.” It wasn’t until the early ’90s that doctors started recommending folic acid use before and during pregnancy to prevent neural tube defects.
paragraphs.zh: 在西方医学领域，包括美国妇产科学院在内的多家权威医学机构于1983年发布了围产期照护的首批指南，明确指出“成为父母的准备应当在受孕前就开始”。直到20世纪90年代初，医生们才开始建议在怀孕前和怀孕期间补充叶酸，以预防神经管缺陷。

paragraphs.en: Social media, it seems, has turbo-charged preconception health from a quick pharmacy stop for prenatals to a full blown industry. In 2019, a study from Harvard went viral because it suggested that, metabolically, carrying a baby was the equivalent to running more than a dozen marathons. Cut to 2025, and “pregnancy prep” accounts have their own vibrant ecosystem. Women seemed to recognize, “Hey, I have to sign up to run a marathon every single day, maybe I want to train for that,” says Radway. “I would want to be in shape and be well nourished and maybe have a coach and be prepared.”
paragraphs.zh: 社交媒体似乎把孕前保健从“去药店买片孕期维生素”这种简短行为，推向了一个完整的产业。2019年，一项哈佛大学的研究在网络上走红，因为它指出，在代谢层面上，怀孕相当于跑完十几场马拉松。到了2025年，“怀孕准备”账号形成了自己生机勃勃的生态系统。拉德韦说：“女性似乎意识到，‘喂，我得每天去准备一次马拉松训练，也许我该为此做更充分的训练。’”“我想要保持状态、营养充足，甚至请一个教练来指导并做好准备。”

paragraphs.en: Radway started posting on Instagram after a difficult first pregnancy led her to get a degree in functional nutritional therapy, which emphasizes holistic dietary and lifestyle changes to address root causes of health issues. As a result, she says that her second pregnancy was a complete 180 from her first. And she felt a “divine calling” to share what she learned with other women. “To me that was the most pressing problem in the world, and I felt like I was uniquely positioned to solve it.”
paragraphs.zh: 拉德韦在第一次怀孕经历困难之后，开始在Instagram发帖，并因此去攻读功能性营养治疗学位，这一学科强调通过整体饮食和生活方式的调整来处理健康问题的根源。她说，由此她的第二次怀孕与第一次相比发生了“彻底的180度转变”。并且她感到自己有“神圣的使命”去与其他女性分享自己的所学。她说：“对我来说，那是世界上最迫切的问题，而我也觉得自己独特地站在了能够解决它的位置上。”

paragraphs.en: The current fixation on optimizing this fragile, sensitive zero trimester arrives at a moment when the average woman is having children three years later than their mothers did—if they have them at all. Though there is an emerging discussion around declining sperm counts thanks to a pivotal study in 2017—spawning a sperm-obsessed optimization movement—the latest stats suggest one in five women will experience infertility. And the burden of infertility, historically, has always fallen on women, even though this new data reveals a more complex reality.
paragraphs.zh: 当前对优化这段脆弱、敏感的“零妊娠期”的执念，恰逢其时：当下的女性平均比上一代母辈晚了三年才生育——前提是她们最终会选择生育。尽管2017年一项里程碑研究引发了关于精子数量下降的讨论，并催生了对“精子优化”的狂热，但最新统计显示，每五位女性中就有一位会经历不孕。而且，历史上生育力不足的负担始终落在女性身上，即使这些新数据揭示了更复杂的现实。

paragraphs.en: Unsurprisingly, one recent report suggests nearly three-quarters of Gen Z have fertility anxiety. The generation grew up with unlimited access to information about the ups and downs of conception and pregnancy, including infertility tell-alls and Girl Boss “Why I froze my eggs” articles; wearables that track macros, movement, and menstrual cycles; and a MAHA movement that has stoked fears about environmental toxins and falsely promotes the idea that moms taking Tylenol might give their babies autism and ADHD. Pair all of this with the extreme rollback of America’s reproductive rights since Roe’s fall in 2022 (17 states either have a total abortion ban or make it illegal after six weeks, making it difficult to get life-saving care) and you have fertile soil for a pregnancy prep fixation to take root.
paragraphs.zh: 并不令人意外，最近有一份报告显示，几乎四分之三的Z世代女性都对生育力焦虑。该代人从小就能获取关于受孕与怀孕起伏的海量信息，包括不孕揭露类内容和《Girl Boss》杂志上“我为什么冷冻了卵子”这类文章；能使用可穿戴设备追踪宏量营养、运动和月经周期；还能接触到MAHA运动，这一运动煽动了对环境毒素的恐惧，并错误宣扬“妈妈服用泰诺会让孩子患自闭症和多动症/注意缺陷障碍”。再叠加自2022年“罗伊案”被推翻以来美国生育权的极端倒退——17个州要么实行全面堕胎禁令，要么在妊娠六周后即属非法，使得获得危及生命的医疗救助都变得困难——就为备孕执着提供了极易生根发芽的土壤。

paragraphs.en: In response, younger generations are saying: “I ought to be able to have a pregnancy which is healthy and safe and happy, and I ought to get the support I need to be able to do that,” explains Lara Freidenfelds, a historian and author of The Myth of the Perfect Pregnancy: A History of Miscarriage in America.
paragraphs.zh: 对此，较年轻的一代在呼吁：‘我应该能够拥有一次健康、安全、幸福的怀孕，而我也理应得到完成这件事所需的支持’，《美国流产神话：流产史中的“完美怀孕”》的作者、历史学家拉拉·弗里登菲尔兹解释道。

paragraphs.en: “We're just a bit tired of it,” says UK-based Sophie Payne, 33, of women not getting helpful answers about their health issues. At 27, Payne was diagnosed with premature ovarian failure and struggled to conceive. In 2022, she suffered multiple miscarriages. While there’s no “one thing” that helped her conceive in 2023, she believes that working with functional health professionals to change up her diet and lifestyle helped. Payne started a holistic pregnancy prep Instagram account called Curious_Clementine with her sister in early 2024. “Sadly, when it comes to getting pregnant, we're not really told what to do, how to do that, and how to go into it in the best possible health for us, but also for our future children.”
paragraphs.zh: “我们只是有点累了。”住在英国、33岁的索菲·佩恩（Sophie Payne）表示，女性在健康问题上得不到有用答案让她感到厌倦。在27岁时，佩恩被诊断为早发性卵巢功能衰竭，并长期挣扎于备孕。到2022年，她经历了多次流产。尽管没有“单一因素”让她在2023年成功受孕，但她认为，借助功能医学从业者调整饮食和生活方式起到了帮助作用。佩恩于2024年初与姐姐一起开设了名为Curious_Clementine的全方位备孕Instagram账号。她说：“可悲的是，当涉及到怀孕时，我们并没有被明确告诉该做什么、如何去做，以及如何让自己处于最佳健康状态去迎接怀孕，不仅为了我们自己，也为了我们的未来孩子。”

paragraphs.en: In the not-so-distant past, moms-to-be would have been taught about pregnancy and childbirth from elders and other women in their community. These days, they live in an increasingly isolated world, often far from grandparents and mothers. They try their best to figure things out as they go—and often social media is their greatest mentor. From the Paynes’ view, more women want to take control of their health, which is one big element driving this trend. “They want to do their own research and do what they can to feel their best,” Payne says. The sisters estimate about half of the women who reach out to them have struggled to get pregnant or had a miscarriage and are looking for simple lifestyle tweaks that could help. After all, a few nutritional swaps and supplements cost a lot less than IUI or IVF.
paragraphs.zh: 在并不遥远的过去，准妈妈通常会向长辈和社区里的其他女性学习孕期和分娩知识。如今，她们生活在一个日益孤立的世界里，往往远离了祖父母和母亲。她们只能尽力边走边学，而社交媒体常常成了她们最重要的“导师”。在佩恩看来，越来越多女性希望掌控自己的健康，是推动这一趋势的关键因素之一。“她们想自己做研究，尽可能做到最好，让自己状态更好，”佩恩说。两位姐妹估计，大约一半来咨询她们的女性，都曾在备孕中受挫或经历过流产，正在寻找一些简单的生活方式调整来帮助自己。毕竟，与其说做一轮IUI或IVF，几项饮食替换和补充剂要便宜得多。

paragraphs.en: Rohr, as one of 10 children with 29 nieces and nephews, has watched countless family members and friends navigate hard pregnancies. In response, she’s determined to have a positive, empowering one. “I always thought having a baby was, like, the least casual thing ever,” she says. “It just seems like this life-changing thing that I wanted to be super, super sure about.”
paragraphs.zh: 罗尔是10个孩子的母亲，也是这位“29位外甥侄女”的阿姨，她看着无数家人和朋友经历过艰难的孕期。于是她立志要拥有一个积极、有力量的孕期经历。“我一直认为生一个孩子是，真的、真的很不随意的事情，”她说。“那是一件能改变人生的大事，我想对自己很有把握再去做。”

paragraphs.en: Doctors say that, in general, all this new attention surrounding the “zero trimester” is a very positive, exciting development. Healthy moms usually spell better outcomes for mom and baby. Currently, the American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists recommend doctors ask patients of reproductive age if they plan to get pregnant within the year during checkups. “There's so many things that we can do to optimize underlying health in that preconception year that will make outcomes in pregnancy better,” says Natalie Clark Stentz, an ob-gyn and reproductive endocrinologist and infertility specialist at Michigan Medicine. This is especially true if you have a chronic health condition, like diabetes, hypertension, depression, or a thyroid disease, that needs to be managed and monitored during pregnancy.
paragraphs.zh: 医生表示，整体而言，目前围绕“零孕期”产生的这股热度是一种非常积极、令人振奋的发展。健康的妈妈通常意味着母婴结局更好。美国妇产科学院目前建议，医生在体检时应询问育龄患者在未来一年内是否计划怀孕。密歇根医学院的产科妇产科医生、 生殖内分泌和不孕不育专家娜塔莉·克拉克·斯滕茨（Natalie Clark Stentz）说，“在准备怀孕这一年里，我们其实有许多可以做的事来优化基础健康，这能让孕期结局更好。”这在你有慢性健康问题时尤其重要，比如糖尿病、高血压、抑郁症或甲状腺疾病，这些问题在怀孕期间都需要管理和监测。

paragraphs.en: At the same time, that “prep” should be expert-vetted and backed by science, and it usually doesn’t involve the TikTok Shop. A doctor’s preconception toolbox is much simpler than what you might see online, and really hasn’t changed much in decades: ensure vaccinations are up to date, avoid alcohol, stop smoking and taking drugs, start a prenatal vitamin with folic acid to prevent neural tube defects at least a month before getting pregnant, and go through any prescription medications and supplements with your doctor. Only 5 percent of the preconception nutritional claims on social media reviewed in a 2025 study were referred to in current international preconception guidelines, and 54 percent were considered to have “no evidence for the health outcome.” TikTok and Instagram had a higher percentage of “no evidence” claims than other platforms.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，这种“备孕”应当经过专家审核并有科学依据，而且通常不涉及“TikTok Shop”。医生的备孕工具箱比你在线上看到的要简单得多，而且几十年来几乎没怎么变：确保疫苗接种更新到位，避免饮酒，停止吸烟和使用毒品，至少在怀孕前一个月开始补充叶酸的孕前维生素，以预防神经管缺陷，并与医生一起核对所有处方药和补充剂。2025年一项研究审查了社交媒体上的备孕营养宣称，只有5%的内容被写入当前国际备孕指南，而有54%被认为“对健康结局没有证据支持”。TikTok和Instagram上“无证据”宣称的比例，高于其他平台。

paragraphs.en: For instance, raw milk is a darling of self-proclaimed “crunchy moms,” yet unpasteurized milk can introduce harmful germs like listeria, which can cause a miscarriage or harm a fetus. Extreme diets and exercise can work against your fertility, too, by affecting the hormones that are necessary for conception, says Kara Goldman, an ob-gyn and associate professor of reproductive endocrinology and infertility and director of fertility preservation at Northwestern University. Recently, a patient with a history of estrogen-receptor-positive breast cancer told her she’d been taking beef organ supplements, not realizing until Goldman dug into the ingredients that the capsules included “ovary” and “uterus.” This meant she was inadvertently taking supplemental estrogen after years of avoiding unnecessary estrogen exposure. Additionally, organ meats like liver can be rich in Vitamin A, which Stentz says can be “very toxic” for pregnancy.
paragraphs.zh: 例如，未经巴氏杀菌的生牛奶是许多自称“极简健康妈妈”或“追求自然妈妈”的宠儿，但未巴氏消毒的牛奶可能带来致病菌，如李斯特菌，这种细菌可能导致流产或损害胎儿。卡拉·戈尔德曼（Kara Goldman）说，极端饮食和运动也可能损害你的生育力，因为它们会影响受孕所必需的激素。她是妇产科医生、西北大学生殖内分泌与不孕症副教授兼生育力保留项目主任。最近，一位既往有雌激素受体阳性乳腺癌病史的患者告诉她自己一直在服用牛肉脏器补充剂，直到戈尔德曼检查成分才意识到这些胶囊里含有“卵巢”和“子宫”。这意味着她在多年刻意避免不必要的雌激素暴露后，无意中又开始补充额外雌激素。此外，像肝脏这样的内脏肉含有大量维生素A，斯坦茨（Stentz）表示，这对怀孕而言“可能非常有毒”。

paragraphs.en: “Any buzzy individual thing is likely sensational, whether that's Brazil nuts, organ meats, or whatnot,” Stentz adds. “The evidence-based things, they're not sexy. Maintain a normal BMI, stop smoking, pick a boring prenatal vitamin.”
paragraphs.zh: 斯坦茨补充说，任何热门的“神奇”单一做法都可能夸张——不管是巴西坚果、内脏肉，还是其他东西。她说，真正有依据的建议并不“性感”：维持正常体重指数、戒烟、选择一款“普通”的产前维生素。

paragraphs.en: Pregnancy prep regimens can get pricey fast. A month’s supply of Perelel’s “conception support pack,” which includes a prenatal, omega DHA + EPA, and CoQ12 + folate, costs $58.77. A full swap-out of all kitchen Tupperwares, cooking utensils, and pans can run you hundreds. Add on “soft movement” like Pilates, organic produce, a whole new set of makeup and skin-care products, and it becomes all the more expensive.
paragraphs.zh: 备孕方案很快就会变得昂贵。佩瑞莱尔（Perelel）的“受孕支持套装”每月售58.77美元，其中包括一款产前维生素、Omega DHA + EPA 和辅酶Q12+叶酸。全面更换厨房里的提盒、厨具和平底锅就可能花几百美元。再加上“温和运动”比如普拉提、有机农产品、一整套新的化妆与护肤用品，花费会更高。

paragraphs.en: In a recent post, Radway explicitly calls out to “the woman looking for a sign to start preparing her body for pregnancy in 2026,” promoting her January Jumpstart, a “7-day winter reset to prepare your body, mind, and soul for pregnancy and birth.” For $97 you can get her Baby Ready Body Book Bundle, and in December, for $1,770, she offered her “Baby Ready Body LIVE” courses that promise to “balance your hormones, boost your energy, calm your nervous system, and prepare your body for a healthy pregnancy.” The courses promise to not only optimize hormone health and cook nutrient-dense meals, but also “prepare your body to give your baby the best possible start.”
paragraphs.zh: 在一篇近期文章中，拉德威（Radway）明确呼吁“正在寻找在2026年开始为怀孕做身体准备信号的女性”，并推广她的1月“Jumpstart”，这是一个“7天冬季重启计划”，用于为怀孕和分娩准备你的身体、思维与心理状态。只要97美元，你就可以购买她的《Baby Ready Body》电子书套装；在12月，她以1,770美元推出了“Baby Ready Body LIVE”课程，课程宣称可以“平衡激素、提升能量、平静神经系统，并为健康妊娠做好身体准备”。这些课程不仅承诺优化激素健康、烹饪营养密度高的餐食，还声称“帮助你的身体为宝宝提供尽可能最好的起点”。

paragraphs.en: “You're taking a very vulnerable, very highly motivated population of patients and targeting them with information that is kind of driven by financial incentives,” says Goldman.
paragraphs.zh: “你是在针对一群非常脆弱、且高度动机化的患者群体，向他们提供由财务激励驱动的信息，”高尔德曼说。

paragraphs.en: The marketing can disguise the fact that even going into pregnancy in peak health is not a guarantee. For starters, we have “so little control over whether or not we're able to become pregnant or how easily we're able to become pregnant,” says Stentz. Early pregnancy loss is “very, very common and unpreventable for the most part,” adds Freidenfelds. “It is hard for people to manage the idea that you will try very hard to reduce risk, and yet you will not be able to completely forestall all bad outcomes.”
paragraphs.zh: 这种营销会掩盖一个事实：即使是在身体状态最佳的情况下进入怀孕期，也并不意味着一定会顺利怀孕。斯滕茨说：“首先，我们几乎无法控制自己是否能怀孕，也无法控制自己怀孕得有多容易。”弗赖登费尔茨补充道，早期流产“在很大程度上是非常、非常常见且几乎无法预防的”。“人们很难接受一个现实：你会尽最大努力降低风险，但仍然无法完全避免所有不理想的结果。”

paragraphs.en: This hyper-focus on women’s health can also subtly shift the burden of infertility, miscarriage, and fetal health onto their shoulders. In reality, men’s biology plays a role in 30 to 40 infertility cases. Under an Instagram post announcing a new book called, 9 Months That Count Forever: How your pregnancy diet shapes your baby’s future, by French biochemist Jessie Inchauspé, “the Glucose Goddess,” one commenter wrote, “I feel so much guilt for not eating well during pregnancy. Definitely a bit triggered by this …” Another typed, “Well I threw up sometimes 10X a day until she was born so … Prayers.”
paragraphs.zh: 这种对女性健康的过度聚焦，也会把不孕、流产和胎儿健康的负担悄悄转移到女性肩上。实际上，男性生物学因素在30%到40%的不孕案例中起作用。在法国生物化学家杰西·英肖佩（Jessie Inchauspé，“葡萄糖女神”）新书《9 Months That Count Forever: How your pregnancy diet shapes your baby’s future》发布的一则Instagram帖子下，一位评论者写道：“我在怀孕期间吃得不太好，感觉非常有负罪感。这个确实让我有点触动……”另一位则输入道：“嗯，我在她出生前有时候一天呕吐十次，所以……祝福。”

paragraphs.en: “It really can make women feel guilty or blame-worthy if their outcome isn't ‘perfect,’ however they're defining perfect,” says Waggoner, an associate professor of sociology at Rice University. This trend promotes the idea that there’s a “causal and deterministic link between preconception care behaviors and birth outcomes, and that's where I think it can be problematic for both individuals and at a policy level.”
paragraphs.zh: “这确实会让女性在结果并不‘完美’——不论‘完美’是如何定义的——时感到内疚或自我归咎，”赖斯大学社会学副教授瓦格纳说。她指出，这一趋势强化了一个观念：将孕前保健行为与分娩结果之间存在“因果且决定论式的联系”，她认为这在个人层面和政策层面都可能带来问题。

paragraphs.en: Perhaps most importantly, Goldman wants to set the record straight: Infertility is a disease, and most causes can't be prevented or treated by lifestyle modifications. Over the past 25 years, Freidenfelds has watched fertility get reframed as something less biologically focused, and instead, something you can plan or try to “do well.” When she started researching her book in the early aughts, the term “trying to conceive,” or “TTC,” was reserved for specialty internet forums for women with infertility. By 2018, women started to throw the term around when they were simply referring to stopping birth control the next month to try to get pregnant. “That's a very, very different scenario,” she says.
paragraphs.zh: 更重要的是，Goldman想把这个事实说清楚：不孕不育是一种疾病，而且多数成因并不能通过生活方式调整来预防或治疗。过去25年里，Freidenfelds观察到，人们对生育力的理解越来越偏离生物医学层面，反而被重新定义为一种可以“安排”或“努力做好”的事情。她在2000年代初开始研究本书时，“trying to conceive”（“TTC”）这个词，几乎只在一些面向不孕女性的专业网络论坛中出现。到了2018年，许多女性在只是“下个月停用避孕药，准备怀孕”时也会直接用这个词。她说：“这是两个非常、非常不同的情境。”

paragraphs.en: Rohr doesn’t yet know if she will have a hard time getting pregnant, but that hasn’t stopped her from sharing her preparation with the world. In May of 2025, she began posting behind the scenes of her own journey on TikTok, including one video titled “POV me and my husband decided to lock tf in and get fit before trying to get pregnant so I can have a healthy (& easier) pregnancy and our baby can have the best chance at a healthy start.” The post went viral, racking up over 13.5 million views and 2.5 million likes.
paragraphs.zh: Rohr目前还不知道自己是否会在受孕上遇到困难，但这并没有阻止她把自己的准备过程分享给大众。2025年5月，她开始在TikTok上发布自己备孕之路的幕后内容，其中一条视频标题是：“POV me and my husband decided to lock tf in and get fit before trying to get pregnant so I can have a healthy (& easier) pregnancy and our baby can have the best chance at a healthy start.”（“你可以把它理解为我和丈夫决定先把目标定下来、先做好体能，再去尝试怀孕，这样我能拥有更健康、更轻松的孕期，也让我们宝宝有更好的健康起点机会。”）这条内容迅速走红，获得了超过1350万次观看和250万次点赞。

paragraphs.en: Rohr knows that some of her “hacks” might seem bizarre or controversial. In fact, she started posting an entire series called “unhinged things we’re doing to prep for conception,” because her friends were fascinated by it. She even recently read a book called Hunt, Gather, Parent, geared towards parenting young children.
paragraphs.zh: Rohr知道自己有些“技巧”看起来可能很怪异，甚至有争议。事实上，她最近开了一个完整系列，叫做 “unhinged things we’re doing to prep for conception”（“我们为了备孕而做的离谱做法”），因为她的朋友对这些内容很着迷。她最近还读了一本叫做《Hunt, Gather, Parent》的书，主要面向幼儿养育阶段的父母。

paragraphs.en: “The energy [in our home] is just like, all pointed in one direction,” Rohr says. “Not knowing things is very uncomfortable for me.” If all goes well, she hopes to have a baby by this Christmas.
paragraphs.zh: Rohr说：“我们家里的精力都被集中在一个方向。”“我非常不舒服于不确定的状态。”如果一切顺利，她希望在今年圣诞节前后迎来宝宝。

paragraphs.en: What Say You?Let us know what you think about this article in the comments below. Alternatively, you can submit a letter to the editor at [email protected].
paragraphs.zh: 你怎么看？请在下方评论区告诉我们对这篇文章的看法。或者，你也可以向编辑写信到 [email protected]。
